hypothesis: If natural-language structural-color prompts are encoded by a frozen LLM and decoded by a small transformer over a discrete material–thickness vocabulary trained on simulated spectra, then the generated multilayer thin-film stacks will match target colors with low CIE76 ΔE (including for paraphrases and unseen targets) and accuracy will improve with larger encoders/decoders because pretrained language features combined with physics-constrained tokens restrict decoding to feasible optical solutions.
